tips: use NAT precision for models in any native format
use source as key-handler for workers/model-processing
tags can be added to each scope case independently
for IR models framework defines source model format
currently used tags:
llm-weekly-optimum - weekly (P1) model scope to be converted with optimum-intel
weekly-custom - weekly (P1) model scope to be converted with custom ovc settings
llm-rest-optimum - rest model scope (P2) scope - the result of subtracting the weekly (P1) from the whole scope
llm-hf-optimum - models for HuggingFace OpenVINO publication
llm-ovms - models validated as a part of OVMS
vllm-optimum - models validated as a part of vLLM
llm-nightly - models validated in nightly
llm-embed - models validated using text embedding pipeline
llm-rerank - models validated using text reranking pipeline
npu - models tested on NPU
npu-exclude-wcl - models to be excluded from WCL machines
llm-unsupported - models that will be supported within some period of time and were kept instead of temporary removal
modelscope - indicates model whose native source is https://modelscope.cn/ - the Chinese equivalent of the HuggingFace
mscf - models for Microsoft WCR scope
llm-deprecated - models removed from validation scope, kept for further complete removal during cleanup
transformers>5.0.0 - models validated on transformers version above 5.0.0 based on optimum-intel model configuration
transformers==4.57.6 - models validated on transformers version 4.57.6 based on optimum-intel model configuration
transformers==4.53.3 - models validated on transformers version 4.53.3 based on optimum-intel model configuration
transformers==4.53.1 - models validated on transformers version 4.53.1 based on optimum-intel model configuration
transformers==5.2.0 - models validated on transformers version 5.2.0 based on optimum-intel model configuration
transformers==5.5.4 - models validated on transformers version 5.5.4 based on optimum-intel model configuration
llm-memory - scope for memory use
